title: Makefile: install targets independent of build
body:
Building from source would involve separate `make` and `make install`
steps.

This removes a lot of unnecessary `-nobuild` targets which were
otherwise needed for packaging.

Signed-off-by: Lokesh Mandvekar <lsm5@fedoraproject.org>

@mheon @baude @rhatdan @vrothberg @edsantiago @cevich PTAL

/cc @containers/podman-maintainers

<!--
Thanks for sending a pull request!

Please make sure you've read our contributing guidelines and how to submit a pull request (https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/main/CONTRIBUTING.md#submitting-pull-requests).

In case you're only changing docs, make sure to prefix the pull-request title with "[CI:DOCS]".  That will prevent functional tests from running and save time and energy.

Finally, be sure to sign commits with your real name.  Since by opening
a PR you already have commits, you can add signatures if needed with
something like `git commit -s --amend`.
-->

I'm gonna try to cleanup the older `.copr` stuff in this pr.
@lsm5 RPM test isn't happy.  I don't see anything off hand to tilt it over though, but not my area of expertise.

> @lsm5 RPM test isn't happy. I don't see anything off hand to tilt it over though, but not my area of expertise.

yup, I need to further adjust Makefile along with that CI job.

@cevich for the `Test build RPM` test, I have changed  `make package` to use `rpkg local` which uses the file `podman.spec.rpkg`. This will need the package `rpkg` to be installed on the CI env. Should that be going in the image itself, or somewhere in the CI setup config in the podman repo?

> Should that be going in the image itself, or somewhere in the CI setup config in the podman repo?

Thanks for asking.  The images are fairly bloated, and if I'm not mistaken this is one job for just this repo, ya?  Also, I'm imagining this `rpkg` tool (being newer than rpmbuild) will likely change/update more frequently.  So at the risk of the occasional install-related flake, I'd say go ahead and install it at runtime for now (e.g. in `setup_environment.sh`).  If the situation changes, the tool is needed in every repo., and/or this strategy proves an unreliable/flaky strategy then we can include it into the image.  Sound reasonable?

> > Should that be going in the image itself, or somewhere in the CI setup config in the podman repo?
> 
> Thanks for asking. The images are fairly bloated, and if I'm not mistaken this is one job for just this repo, ya? Also, I'm imagining this `rpkg` tool (being newer than rpmbuild) will likely change/update more frequently. So at the risk of the occasional install-related flake, I'd say go ahead and install it at runtime for now (e.g. in `setup_environment.sh`). If the situation changes, the tool is needed in every repo., and/or this strategy proves an unreliable/flaky strategy then we can include it into the image. Sound reasonable?

Yup, works for now. Eventually we may need it in more repos if we wanna provide copr builds for each package on every PR merge. But that's for later. Thanks a lot!
Yep, certainly if it's used far and wide, let's get it into the images.
@cevich is CI stuck or something? This flake https://cirrus-ci.com/task/5633459950452736 went green on re-run, but other tests don't seem to be running.
hang on a sec...need to carefully review the multi-arch build changes...
Okay, confirmed the multi-arch binaries are correctly built.  I checked by running the `file` command on the contents of the `s390x` tarball.
